channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump meets with congressional Republicans as GOP lawmakers argue over tax and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted an unusually long meeting with House Republicans at the White House on Thursday, turning over prime workspace for them to hammer out differences over the size, scope and details of their multitrillion-dollar plan to cut taxes, regulations and governmen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over a dozen Venezuelan criminal illegal migrants sent to Guantanamo Bay: 'Highly dangerous' people
item.description: Department of Homeland Security sources confirmed that 13 Venezuelans are being flown to Guantánamo Bay Detention Camp in Cuba, some of whom are members of the violent Tren de Aragua criminal organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HUD secretary directs agency to ban transgender women from federally funded women-only shelters
item.description: Scott Turner, Trump's recently confirmed HUD Secretary, said he is directing agency staff to halt any pending or future enforcement action related to the Obama-era equal access rule that required HUD funded programs and shelters to determine eligibility based on a person's stated gender-id
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS workers involved in 2025 tax season can't take buyout until after the taxpayer filing deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — IRS employees involved in the 2025 tax season will not be allowed to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration until after the taxpayer filing deadline, according to a letter sent Wednesday to IRS employees.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota House DFL, GOP strike agreement to end stalemate
item.description: A standoff that has stretched on for more than three weeks is ending in the Minnesota House. A deal will get the chamber up and running.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Republicans in Congress mostly shrug as Musk and DOGE set sights on spending
item.description: Congress controls the power of the purse, but Republicans on Capitol Hill have put up little resistance to efforts by the administration to suspend spending that they've already approved.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Nassau police to help ICE detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes
item.description: NASSAU COUNTY, N.Y. (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida immigration bill, marijuana petition spark debate
item.description: The battle between Gov. Ron DeSantis and state GOP lawmakers has become more public and continues to heat up.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
